title: RFC 9000 Address-Validation Token Emission Verification
verification_method: Focused unit tests plus runtime inspection around path-validation transitions and NEW_TOKEN frame formatting.
expected_result: Server-role validation of a new address emits a protected NEW_TOKEN datagram exactly once per address, client-role validation does not emit NEW_TOKEN traffic, and the internal helper formats a valid NEW_TOKEN frame payload that round-trips through the codec.
procedure: Run the two requirement-home tests for the token-emission slice.
Confirm the helper payload round-trips through QuicFrameCodec.TryParseNewTokenFrame.
Confirm repeated validation of the same address does not create a second NEW_TOKEN send effect.
scope: This slice covers server-only address-validation token emission and the proof that repeated validation transitions do not create duplicate NEW_TOKEN traffic. It does not claim Retry-token generation or client-side token handling.
status_summary: This verification artifact records the landed address-validation token-emission slice and points at the requirement-home and runtime evidence in the working tree.
preconditions: The RFC 9000 token-emission requirement homes are discoverable by the test runner.
The runtime keeps server-only NEW_TOKEN emission internal to the repository.
